artifact_name_en: Jade Hairpin inscribed signature Zigang with chi tiger pattern
ocr_text:
明晚期
「子剛」款螭紋玉簪
Jade Hairpin inscribed signature Zigang with chi tiger pattern
Late Ming dynasty
1522 - 1644 C.E.
caption: 子剛款螭紋玉簪名稱牌

ocr_text:
陸子剛以「工緻倣古」的特色，受文人雅士的稱頌。方志、筆記中記載他用昆吾刀刻玉，但歸納實物，子剛仍是以解玉砂琢磨玉器的形、紋，只是落款以刀刻。其風格不僅在當時蔚為流行，還出現大量的冒名之作，甚至到二十世紀仍不斷仿造。
Lu Zigang was best known for his "fine workmanship and archaic style" and for this feature much admired by the literati class. Local chronicles and literati notes said he worked his jades with the legendary kunwu knife. Judging from the actual works of his, however, we know that the forms and patterns were done by the conventional method, jieyusha, and only his signature was carved out by knife work. The popularity of Style Zigang extended beyond its time and imitations or fakes abounded, well into the 20th century.
caption: 兩件方形白玉盒與一件玉碗陳列櫃內，左側為刻款拓片，背景為陸子剛款玉器說明牆
notes: 展品與展區介紹文字同框

gallery_guess: 玉器展廳-各種服飾用玉
ocr_text:
各種服飾用玉
春秋、戰國之交，由於服飾的改變，束腰的帶鉤開始流行。造形、紋飾變化多端。漢代時更流行以盤蟠為造形的帶環。帶扣則是受到鮮卑服飾影響下的帶頭。
「韘」(she)本是射箭時套於大拇指上的皮套，目的是避免拉弓弦時割傷拇指。商、西周時就偶見用美玉雕琢的「韘」，又稱為「射決」。使用時要在內爾填塞織物。春秋時玉韘逐漸普及，增加了彎鉤。戰國開始，玉韘逐漸脫離實用性，朝向扁平化、裝飾化發展，當時文獻多稱之為「玦」。
「鉛卯」、「勝形佩」是流行於東漢，具有辟邪意義的佩飾。後者又名「司南佩」。
Ornamental Jades
At the turn of the Spring and Autumn Period and the Warring States Period, belt hooks in various styles and patterns gained popularity because of changes in the way people dressed. Later during Han dynasty belt loops in the shape of coiling chi tiger were fashionable while under the influence of the ethnic Xianbei costume buckles also came into use.
She was a protective leather gear worn on the thumb to avoid bowstring cuts during archery. There were occasionally jade thumb rings from as early as Shang and Western Zhou dynasties, also known as shejue, which had to be stuffed with fabric before use. The use of jade shes became popular during the Spring and Autumn Period, added with a curved hook. Then they went flat and ornamental, gradually removed from practical use, commonly referred to as jue in the period writings.
Cubic gangmao amulets or sheng-shaped pei ornaments, both worn as pendants, were in vogue during Eastern Han dynasty, the latter also called sinan.
caption: 「各種服飾用玉」展區大段中英文介紹牆，說明帶鉤、玉韘、剛卯、勝形佩等服飾用玉
